lessons.title: The European Parliament
lessons.description: We now turn to the institutions of the European Union.

lessons.title: The Council and European Council
lessons.description: Both are intergovernmental in character — representing the interests of member state governments — but they are distinct institutions with very different compositions, functions, and legal roles.

lessons.title: The European Commission
lessons.description: The Commission is unique in the EU's institutional architecture.

lessons.title: The Court of Justice
lessons.description: In terms of its constitutional impact on the EU legal order, the Court of Justice may be the single most important institution of the Union.

lessons.title: Democratic Deficit and Institutional Accountability
lessons.description: This concept refers to the perceived gap between the legislative and executive power exercised by EU institutions and the degree of direct democratic accountability to which those institutions are subject.
